w:r.w:t: Every criterion is explicitly addressed. The table below maps each rubric item to the phase responsible for delivering it.
VLM / multimodal tagging idea has been deliberately cut. It scores zero rubric points, mocks data for the demo, and risks consuming 20+ minutes. Do not re-introduce it.
Complete these steps before the hackathon
timer begins. None of these count toward your 105 minutes but all of them are blocking dependencies.
project at supabase.com. Note your Project URL and
Create a new GitHub repo named '
'. Initialize with a README. Copy the clone URL.
Confirm Python 3.10+ is installed locally: python --version
Install all dependencies in one shot — see the exact pip command below.
to download model weights on first import. Do this NOW, not during the hackathon.
Run this single command to install everything:
the output is NOT 128, do not proceed. Your
128) SQL will crash on every insert. Only continue once you have confirmed the dimension.
Create this folder layout before starting:
SQL Editor (Dashboard → SQL Editor → New Query). Execute each block separately and confirm no errors before proceeding.
CREATE EXTENSION IF NOT EXISTS vector;
Step 0.2 — Create the Three Core Tables
n) scan with a logarithmic multi-layer graph traversal. For 50,000 faces this is the difference between a 2-second query and an instant one. Mention it explicitly in your README.
This Postgres function is what your /
endpoint will call. It does the vector math inside the database, which is far more efficient than pulling all
operator is cosine distance. Subtracting from 1 gives cosine similarity. A threshold of 0.6 is recommended as a starting point (higher = stricter match). Adjust during testing.
This file creates a single shared
client used by all endpoints. Never instantiate the client inside an endpoint function — that leaks connections.
with square brackets (not .get()) so the app crashes immediately at startup if
are missing, rather than failing silently mid-request.
Define explicit response models. This gives
you free Swagger documentation and makes the API structure obvious to judges.
Create this skeleton first. Fill in the endpoint logic in subsequent phases. Do not skip this step — having the structure in place prevents you from getting lost.
recognition image retrieval for large-scale events',
--reload now and confirm http://localhost:8000/health returns {status: ok} and http://localhost:8000/docs shows the Swagger UI. Fix any import errors before proceeding.
This is the highest-value phase of the build. Three rubric criteria are satisfied here. Take the full 30 minutes and do not rush it.
Receive a list of image files via multipart/form-data.
Save each file to a temporary path on disk (
needs a file path, not bytes).
) on each file. This returns a list — one entry per face detected.
For each face detected: generate a UUID (
), store the 128D embedding in the faces table.
Insert the image URL into the images table, then insert one row per face into
Clean up the temp file. Return the
Add this to main.py after the health endpoint:
not faces or all(f['embedding'] == [0.0]*128 for f in faces):
Use the Swagger UI to POST a real face photo to /ingest.
Confirm the response contains a valid
Test an image with two visible faces. Confirm
NOT move to Phase 3 until /ingest works end-to-end and you can see data in
This endpoint accepts a selfie, computes its embedding,
function to find the closest stored
Step 3.3 — Verify the Full End-to-End Flow
Ingest a photo of Person A via POST /ingest. Copy their
Send a second photo of the same Person A to POST /auth.
Confirm the response returns the same
Send a photo of a completely different Person B to POST /auth. Confirm 401 response.
. Confirm their image URL is returned.
If the similarity threshold is causing false negatives (correct person rejected), lower
to 0.5. If causing false positives (wrong person accepted), raise it to 0.65.
Two principles guide this phase: test your business logic, not
. Mock the database so tests run instantly with no external dependencies.
'''Valid image with one face should return one
'''Image with two faces should return two
'''Valid selfie matching a stored face returns
'''Selfie with no DB match returns HTTP 401'''
All 5 tests should pass. Fix any failures before proceeding.
5 passing tests with real business logic assertions is far more impressive than 10 tests that only check HTTP status codes from
The README is your last impression. Keep it tight: setup instructions, architecture explanation, and the HNSW callout. Judges read dozens of these — clarity wins.
## Architecture — a short ASCII diagram (see below) + 2-3 sentences
## Setup — exact commands to clone, install, configure .
## API Reference — table with Method, Endpoint, Description for all 3 routes
## Scale Design — one paragraph explicitly naming HNSW and why it matters for 50k images
README.md is in the repo root and renders correctly on GitHub
HNSW is explicitly mentioned in README under Scale Design
http://localhost:8000/docs is accessible (Swagger UI — free from
} returns images list for a valid
tables have real data from testing
GitHub repo URL is ready to paste into submission form
